design_philosophy.core_loop: Select scenarios, manage territory resources across in-game months, move troops tactically, engage in diplomacy or conquest to unify the island under one family's control
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing territorial expansion through military conquest against diplomatic alliances and resource management; controlling adjacent provinces while managing economy and defense upgrades
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a noble family to dominance over a fantasy island through strategic military campaigns, magical wizard allies, and political maneuvering
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Koei's signature blend of deep turn-based strategy with RPG progression; emphasis on simultaneous multi-front macro-strategy (territory management) paired with tactical micro-management (individual battles); multiple playable families with distinct wizard powers create asymmetric starting positions and encourage replayability through faction-specific narratives

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple selectable scenarios with varying family positions and starting resources; players can choose which family to lead, affecting initial power distribution and available magical units

progression.upgrade_system: Territory economy and defense upgrades accessible via action points each turn; unit diversity (archers, knights, horsemen, wizards, hired units) unlocks through scenario progression and wizard availability
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six wizard gems are distributed among families at game start based on selected scenario; wizard powers tied to family affiliation rather than individual character advancement
replayability_hooks: Four distinct scenarios with different family lineups, vassal configurations, and gem distributions
Six playable families with unique wizard allies granting different magical abilities
Multiplayer mode enabling competitive or cooperative gameplay between human players
Variable map control states encourage different strategic approaches based on starting position

world_building_techniques: Exposition through instruction manual and scenario selection screens explaining the Isle of Ishmeria's history
Mythological foundation: six wizards sealed a Fire Dragon into a ruby crown (Gemfire) to maintain peace
Tyrant king Eselred abused Gemfire's power; Princess Robyn scattered the wizard gems to prevent tyranny
Family and faction names (Blanches, Lyles, Flax, Coryll, Chrysalis, Molbrew) tie to specific wizard patrons
Strategic map positions and resource distribution reflect political power balances between families

faction_indicators: Six noble families competing for throne control
Wizard gem ownership signals magical power and faction identity (Zendor/Lightning for Blanches, Pluvius/Meteors for Lyles)
Princess Robyn as symbolic opposition to tyrannical rule
King Eselred as tyrannical antagonist figure across all scenarios
danger_signals: Tyrannical king Eselred's oppression of subjects
Fire Dragon sealed within Gemfire crown as existential threat
Conflict between family clans seeking throne monopoly
Princess's rebellion against paternal authority highlighting power abuse
environmental_storytelling: Territory control map visually represents which family dominates each region
Castle capture mechanics reinforce military conquest as path to legitimacy
Wizard placement with specific families creates visual power asymmetries
Resource and vassal distribution in scenarios reflects historical power balances
